• Making only the minimum payment every month would result in the repayment stretching over months/years with consequential
compounded interest payment on your outstanding balance.
• You can make the payment of your Credit Card bill through FedMobile Mobile Banking app or Payment Gateway. To know more, please
visit https://www.federalbank.co.in/credit-card-payment-options.
• The "Available Credit Limit" shown in this statement takes into account charges incurred but not due. Please ensure that at least the
'Minimum Amount Due' reaches us by the 'Due Date'.
• If the minimum amount due or part amount less than the total amount due is paid, interest charges are applicable (including fresh
purchases, if any) on an average daily reducing balancing method.
• To hotlist/ block your Credit Card, please use FedMobile (Credit Card module -> Manage Credit Card), FedNet (Credit Card module -
> Manage Credit Card) or mail to creditcards@federalbank.co.in or call 1800 425 1199 or 1800 420 1199 (for resident customers)
and +91-484-2630994 (for NRI customers).
• Late Payment charges will be applicable if Minimum Amount Due is not paid by the payment due date. Clear funds need to be credited
to Federal Bank Credit Card account on or before the payment due date, to avoid Late Payment charges. Late payment will also
result in reporting to Credit Bureau. Credit Bureau include Credit reference Agencies, Credit Information companies or any other entity
formed and authorized by RBI for the purpose of collecting, collating and disseminating credit information pertaining to borrowers.
• Any increase in fees or charges shall be notified in advance to the Cardholders on Federal Bank website https://www.federalbank.co.
in/rates-and-charges. Please logon to https://www.federalbank.co.in to view the "Most Important Terms & Conditions" and updated
information on all ongoing offers.
• In case where Card Member is making remittances from his/her NRO Account, he/she warrants that such remittance is made out of
his/her balances held in the such NRO account arising from his/her legitimate receivables in India and not by the way of borrowings
from any other person or a transfer from any other NRO account. On breach of such warranty, Card Member shall be liable for penal
action under FEMA. Card Member also confirms and warrants that the amount repatriated out of his/her balance in NRO Account to
outside India, amount transferred from NRO account to NRE account, payment towards credit card bill and amount to be transferred
vide any other request is less than USD.1,000,000- (US Dollar one million) or its equivalent in any Bank during a particular financial
year.
• For funds unlisted for Credit Card bill settlement by way of debit to NRO account, Card Member confirms that applicable taxes have
been paid on earnings charged to tax in India as per CBDT rules and Card Member and NRO account holder is one and same.
• As per Network guideline, all card holders should provide a duly filled in Cardholder Dispute Form (CDF) mentioning the details of
the disputed transaction which will enable/authorize the bank to investigate with the respective Merchant/Member Bank. Please
visit our website https://www.federalbank.co.in to view the cardholder dispute form.
• Transaction dispute needs to be reported in writing within 60 days from the statement date in which the said transaction appears.
• When a dispute is raised for a transaction, depending on the nature of the dispute, a temporary credit to the extent of the amount
disputed may be given and an investigation is initiated. This temporary credit will nullify the effect of the disputed transaction on
the total outstanding. This is to ensure that you are not adversely impacted during the period of investigation.
• Based on the outcome of the investigation, the temporary credit may be made permanent resulting in no liability from your end
for the transaction or reversed, wherein the amount under dispute will be debited to your account and payable by you. We will
contact you with details, if the temporary credit given to the card account is reversed. We will send a written communication
advising you about the outcome of your dispute.
